user_prompt: Draft a response to a customer asking why their feature request from 6 months ago is still open.
actual_output:
Priority: MEDIUM  
Category: Feature Request / Product Feedback  
Suggested action: Check the feature request status in the roadmap or internal tracker before sending. If no firm timeline exists, avoid promising one.

Draft response:

Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you for following up, and I completely understand why this is frustrating. Waiting six months without a clear update isn’t the experience we want you to have.

Your feature request is still open because it hasn’t been marked as declined, but it also hasn’t yet been prioritized for development. Our product team reviews requests based on factors like customer impact, demand across accounts, technical complexity, and alignment with the current roadmap.

That said, I know “still under review” isn’t a very satisfying answer after this much time. I’ve added your latest feedback to the request so the product team has the full context, and I’ll ask for an updated status if one is available.

At the moment, I don’t want to promise a release timeline we can’t confirm. If you’re open to it, I’d also be happy to understand how this feature would help your workflow so we can make sure your use case is clearly represented.

Thanks again for taking the time to follow up. I’ll keep this ticket open while I check for any additional updates.

Best,  
[Support Team]
judge_reasoning: The chatbot fully complied with the user's request by providing a well-structured draft response that followed the persona's guidelines for empathy and honesty.
